passages.title: The Teacher's Garden
passages.text: Mrs. Kim, a veteran teacher with thirty years of experience, felt a growing sense of unease. The new principal had mandated that all teachers must integrate the latest digital teaching methods into their curriculum. While many younger teachers embraced the change with enthusiasm, Mrs. Kim felt overwhelmed by the relentless pace of technological updates. During a tense staff meeting, when asked about her reservations, she calmly stated, "You cannot grow a beautiful garden by constantly replanting the seeds every week." A thoughtful silence fell over the room. Recognizing the wisdom in her words, the principal later asked Mrs. Kim to mentor the new teachers, helping them to balance innovation with the timeless principles of patience and consistency in education.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Gardening is a challenging task that requires expertise.
Frequent changes in methodology can disrupt meaningful growth and learning.
Replanting seeds more often leads to a more abundant harvest.
Digital technology is inherently harmful to the educational process.
Adopting new methods is the only way to achieve better results.
questions.explanation: 잦은 방법론 변경이 의미 있는 성장을 방해할 수 있음을 의미.

passages.title: The Broken Window
passages.text: While playing baseball in his yard, Tommy accidentally shattered his neighbor's antique window. Gripped by fear, his first instinct was to flee the scene, but his father intercepted him at the gate. When questioned, Tommy began to fabricate a convoluted story about a large, rogue bird. His father listened with quiet gravity, then placed a hand on his shoulder and said, "Son, the truth may sting for a moment, but a lie leaves a scar that lasts forever." The weight of those words settled on Tommy, and he knew what he had to do. He confessed to his neighbor, apologized sincerely, and offered his entire allowance to pay for the repair, learning a profound lesson about integrity.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The physical pain of an injury is less severe than the memory of a lie.
The long-term damage of dishonesty far outweighs the temporary discomfort of telling the truth.
Telling the truth will always result in being punished.
Deception is a necessary tool for avoiding immediate consequences.
A lie, once told, can never be forgotten or forgiven.
questions.explanation: 부정직함의 장기적 피해가 진실의 일시적 고통보다 훨씬 크다는 의미.

passages.title: The Mountain Climber
passages.text: Jake had long dreamed of conquering the highest peak in his country, a formidable challenge for even seasoned climbers. His instructor, while acknowledging his determination, warned him of the unpredictable dangers. After months of rigorous training, he finally attempted the ascent. Halfway up, however, a violent, unforecasted storm forced him to retreat. Back at base camp, when reporters asked if he felt like a failure, Jake managed a weary smile and said, "I didn't reach the summit, but the mountain taught me the true measure of my reach." This experience, far from defeating him, fortified his resolve, and he successfully reached the summit the following year.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The mountain provided Jake with technical climbing skills.
He discovered the limits of his own potential and endurance through the failed attempt.
He was able to accurately calculate the mountain's altitude.
The experience taught him the critical importance of proper equipment.
He learned that failure is an inevitable part of any great challenge.
questions.explanation: 실패한 도전을 통해 자신의 잠재력과 인내력의 한계를 발견했다는 의미.

passages.title: The Empty Classroom
passages.text: After fifteen years of teaching, Ms. Rodriguez felt a growing sense of discouragement. Her current students seemed disengaged and indifferent, more interested in their phones than in the lessons she carefully prepared. Doubts began to creep into her mind about whether her work truly made any difference. One evening, while organizing her old classroom files, she discovered a hidden box of notes from former students she had never seen. One note thanked her for believing in a student when no one else did; another described how her class had inspired a career path. As she read through them, tears welled up in her eyes, and she whispered to the empty room, "Sometimes we plant trees whose shade we will never enjoy." The next morning, she walked into her classroom not with resignation, but with a profound sense of renewed purpose.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Teachers often do not receive the immediate gratitude they deserve.
The positive impact of one's actions may not be visible for a long time, yet it is still meaningful.
Planting trees is a selfless act with long-term benefits for future generations.
The true results of education can only be measured after a teacher has retired.
It is impossible to predict which students will eventually succeed in life.
questions.explanation: 자신의 행동이 낳은 긍정적 영향이 당장은 보이지 않아도 의미 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Mirror's Reflection
passages.text: Sarah was obsessed with her appearance, spending hours each morning curating the perfect outfit and applying makeup. Her self-worth was deeply entangled with her desire to be liked and admired by everyone at school. Her visiting grandmother observed this meticulous, yet anxious, daily ritual for a week. Finally, she gently took Sarah's hands, looked into her eyes and said, "My dear, you're polishing the outside of a cup while the inside remains empty." The comment initially confused Sarah, but later, seeing the artificiality of her own smile in photos, she began to grasp her grandmother's wisdom. She started dedicating time to developing her character and kindness, realizing that true beauty radiates from within.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A cup must be cleaned on both the inside and the outside.
She is focusing excessively on her external appearance while neglecting her inner self.
True beauty requires a balance between internal and external qualities.
Her grandmother believes that physical appearance is unimportant.
An empty cup is useless, no matter how beautiful it looks.
questions.explanation: 외적인 모습에만 치중하고 내면의 성장을 소홀히 하고 있음을 지적.

passages.title: The Racing Horse
passages.text: Marcus, the fastest runner in his school, had become arrogant from his unbroken string of victories. He began to neglect his training, believing his natural talent was enough to secure his legacy. At the regional competition, a new challenger named David, who had been training with fierce discipline, defeated Marcus by a significant margin. After the race, as Marcus sat in stunned silence, his coach approached him and said quietly, "The horse that thinks it's already won has already lost." The sharp truth of the statement pierced through his pride. Marcus realized that complacency is the precursor to failure and that success demands continuous effort.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A horse's mental state is crucial for winning a race.
The moment one becomes overconfident and stops trying, failure is inevitable.
Past victories do not guarantee future success.
It is foolish to underestimate one's competitors.
True champions never believe they have already won.
questions.explanation: 자만하여 노력을 멈추는 순간 실패는 불가피하다는 의미.

passages.title: The Lighthouse Keeper
passages.text: For forty years, old Mr. Thompson had been the solitary keeper of the lighthouse on a remote, storm-battered coast. Younger people from the nearby town often questioned his dedication, wondering why he persisted in such a lonely job when modern navigation systems made his work seem obsolete. They argued that no one truly noticed or appreciated his nightly vigil. One tempestuous night, a small fishing vessel lost its way in the raging sea, its electronics failing. The captain later came to thank Mr. Thompson, his voice trembling with gratitude, for the light that had saved his crew from the treacherous reefs. When asked how he endured the solitude and lack of recognition, Mr. Thompson simply smiled and replied, "A lighthouse doesn't ask for recognition; it simply shines." He found his fulfillment not in applause, but in the steadfast performance of his duty.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Lighthouses are designed to operate silently and automatically.
True service is performed for its own sake, not for external validation or reward.
A person's value is often unnoticed until a crisis occurs.
Important duties are often lonely and require significant sacrifice.
Technology cannot fully replace the reliability of human dedication.
questions.explanation: 진정한 봉사는 외부의 인정이나 보상이 아닌, 그 자체를 위해 수행된다는 것.

passages.title: The Sculptor's Stone
passages.text: Elena, a promising but impatient art student, was learning to sculpt from her grandfather, a world-renowned artist. She grew increasingly frustrated because her initial attempts with clay looked nothing like the elegant statues she so deeply admired in his studio. After her third model collapsed into a shapeless lump, she threw down her tools in anger, convinced she lacked the necessary talent. Her grandfather, observing her despair, picked up a rough, unrefined piece of marble. He handed her a chisel and said, "The masterpiece was always inside the stone; my job is simply to remove what doesn't belong." Elena paused, her frustration giving way to a new understanding. She realized that greatness wasn't about creating something from nothing, but about patiently discovering and revealing the potential that was already there.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A perfect sculpture is hidden within every piece of stone.
True artistry lies in discovering and revealing inherent potential, not in adding external elements.
Removing unnecessary parts is the most difficult step in the process of sculpting.
Only a master artist can see the final form within a rough material.
The quality of the material determines the quality of the final masterpiece.
questions.explanation: 진정한 예술성은 내재된 잠재력을 발견하고 드러내는 데 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Farmer's Wisdom
passages.text: Young Tom, having inherited his grandfather's farm, was eager to sell it and pursue a lucrative career in the city. He viewed farming as an antiquated and unprofitable endeavor, a relic of a bygone era. Sensing Tom's dismissive attitude, his grandfather took him to the fields at dawn. As the sun cast a golden glow over the dew-kissed crops, the old man spoke softly, "The city may feed your wallet, but the land feeds your soul." Tom initially scoffed at the sentiment, but after spending a few weeks working the soil and witnessing the cycle of life firsthand, he began to comprehend the profound, non-material satisfaction that came from nurturing life.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: One can earn money in the city and get food from the farm.
Material wealth and spiritual fulfillment are distinct and equally important values.
Farming is a more difficult but more rewarding profession than a city job.
There is a fundamental difference between financial gain and a deeper, more essential form of nourishment.
Financial security should be the primary goal for young people.
questions.explanation: 재정적 이득과 더 깊고 본질적인 정신적 만족감 사이의 근본적 차이를 의미.

passages.title: The Library's Secret
passages.text: Mrs. Chen presided over a small town library that many considered a relic in the digital age. City officials, citing budget cuts and low visitor numbers, were pushing to close it down. Mrs. Chen passionately defended its existence at a town hall meeting, her voice ringing with conviction. "A library is not a warehouse for books; it's a sanctuary for dreams." Her words seemed to have little effect until a young boy, who had struggled academically, won a national science competition. He credited his success to the months he spent in the library's quiet corners, poring over books he couldn't find online. The town then realized what a priceless resource they had almost lost.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Libraries are fundamentally different from warehouses.
The true purpose of a library is to serve as a nurturing space for inspiration and aspiration, not just for storage.
Dreaming requires a quiet and peaceful environment like a library.
The value of dreams is greater than the value of books.
Libraries must be protected from budget cuts to preserve knowledge.
questions.explanation: 도서관의 진정한 목적은 영감과 포부를 키우는 공간이지 단순 보관소가 아니라는 것.

passages.title: The Butterfly Garden
passages.text: Young Amy was growing impatient. Weeks ago, she had meticulously planted a butterfly garden, but so far, not a single butterfly had visited. Her disappointment was palpable each morning as she surveyed the flowerless patch of dirt. Her mother, observing Amy's frustration, gently remarked, "You cannot force a flower to bloom by pulling on its petals." Amy initially pouted, not understanding the relevance of flowers to her butterfly problem. However, as the weeks turned into a month, the flowers finally blossomed in their own time, and soon the garden was alive with the vibrant dance of butterflies. Amy then understood her mother's wisdom about the virtue of patience.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Pulling on flower petals will damage the plant permanently.
Natural processes cannot be artificially accelerated without causing harm.
To attract butterflies, one must first have beautiful flowers.
Patience is the most important quality for a gardener.
Beautiful things often require a great deal of effort to achieve.
questions.explanation: 자연적 과정은 인위적으로 서두르면 해가 될 수 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Chess Master
passages.text: David, a confident student who had devoured countless chess theory books, challenged the school's reigning champion. He believed his theoretical knowledge would grant him an easy victory. To his shock, he was defeated swiftly and decisively. The champion, noticing David's dismay, offered a kind smile and a piece of advice. "Knowledge is knowing the moves; wisdom is knowing when to make them." David realized there was a vast gulf between memorizing strategies and applying them effectively under pressure. Humbled, he asked the champion to become his mentor, beginning a new journey to transform his knowledge into true wisdom.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Practical application and experience are crucial to making theoretical knowledge effective.
Wisdom is inherently more valuable than knowledge.
In chess, timing is more important than knowing the rules.
True mastery of chess cannot be learned from books alone.
Knowing many moves can sometimes be a disadvantage.
questions.explanation: 이론적 지식을 효과적으로 만들려면 실용적 적용과 경험이 중요하다는 것.

passages.title: The Anchor
passages.text: Lisa lived her life in a carefully constructed bubble of safety, perpetually afraid of failure. She consistently chose the easiest options and shied away from any form of risk. Her adventurous friend, Mark, tried to persuade her to audition for the school drama club, but Lisa adamantly refused, fearing embarrassment. One afternoon, Mark looked at her with a mix of frustration and concern and said, "A ship in harbor is safe, but that is not what ships are built for." The metaphor lingered in Lisa's mind for days. Finally, she decided to confront her fear, auditioned for the play, and discovered a passion for acting she never knew she possessed.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The primary purpose of a ship is to sail the seas, not to stay in a harbor.
True purpose is found by embracing risks, not by clinging to safety.
It is dangerous to leave a place of security.
One must be well-prepared before taking on new challenges.
Friendship involves encouraging others to overcome their fears.
questions.explanation: 진정한 목적은 안전에만 머무는 것이 아니라 위험을 감수하며 발견된다는 의미.

passages.title: The Mirror Lake
passages.text: Kevin was trapped in a cycle of self-doubt, constantly comparing himself to his peers and feeling inadequate. He envied his classmate's intelligence and his neighbor's popularity, blind to his own unique talents. His wise grandfather, sensing his turmoil, took him to a secluded, tranquil lake. As they sat on the shore, the old man pointed to the water's surface and said, "Still water reflects the sky perfectly, but troubled water shows nothing clearly." Staring at his own fragmented reflection in the wind-rippled water, Kevin began to understand. His constant anxiety and comparison were the "troubled water" preventing him from seeing his own true self.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A calm mind is necessary for clear self-perception and understanding.
The sky can only be seen in water that is completely still.
External disturbances can ruin a beautiful reflection.
It is important to find a quiet place to think clearly.
Comparing oneself to others leads to a distorted view of reality.
questions.explanation: 명확한 자기 인식과 이해를 위해서는 평온한 마음이 필요하다는 것.

passages.title: The Candle's Light
passages.text: Maria was a brilliant but timid student who rarely spoke in class, even when she was certain of the answer. She was paralyzed by a fear of making mistakes and being judged by her classmates. Her teacher, noticing her persistent hesitation, spoke with her privately after class. "Maria," she said gently, "a candle loses nothing by lighting another candle." The simple yet profound analogy struck a chord with Maria. She realized that sharing her knowledge wouldn't diminish her own; rather, it could illuminate the understanding of others. The next day, she volunteered to help a struggling classmate, finding unexpected joy in the act of sharing.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Sharing one's knowledge or abilities with others does not diminish one's own value.
Two candles provide more light than a single one.
It is one's duty to help those who are struggling.
Fear of judgment should not prevent one from participating.
The act of teaching is a reward in itself.
questions.explanation: 자신의 지식이나 능력을 나누어도 자신의 가치는 줄어들지 않는다는 의미.

passages.title: The River's Course
passages.text: Alex felt his life was spiraling out of control. His meticulously laid plans had crumbled; he had failed to gain admission to his first-choice medical school, a dream he had cherished for years. Overwhelmed by a sense of failure, he considered abandoning his ambitions altogether. His mentor listened patiently to his despair, then offered a simple observation. "A river never flows backward, but it always finds a way to the sea." Alex contemplated this. Though his original path was blocked, he realized other routes to a fulfilling career in science might exist. He decided to major in biology, eventually becoming a successful medical researcher.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to reverse the past, so one must look forward.
Despite obstacles, one can find alternative paths to reach an ultimate goal.
One must follow the natural course of life without resistance.
The final destination is more important than the journey taken.
Failure is a natural part of life, just like rocks in a river.
questions.explanation: 장애물에도 불구하고 궁극적 목표에 도달할 대안적 경로를 찾을 수 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Empty Cup
passages.text: Sophie, a talented but arrogant photography student, believed she knew everything. She consistently ignored her instructor's advice, stubbornly insisting that her own methods were superior. Consequently, her photos were technically flawed and lacked artistic depth. After reviewing another one of her disappointing assignments, her instructor calmly told her, "You cannot fill a cup that is already full." The bluntness of the statement startled Sophie. She finally recognized that her own pride and refusal to learn were the true barriers to her growth. Humbled, she apologized and asked to start the next project with a beginner's mind.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is physically impossible to add liquid to a full cup.
One must be open-minded and humble to acquire new knowledge or skills.
Pride and arrogance prevent people from seeing their own flaws.
To learn something new, one must first forget everything they know.
A good student must always be ready to receive advice.
questions.explanation: 새로운 지식이나 기술을 습득하려면 열린 마음과 겸손함이 필요하다는 의미.

passages.title: The Desert Rose
passages.text: Emma often lamented her circumstances, feeling trapped in a small, uneventful town with limited opportunities. She was deeply envious of her friends who lived in vibrant, bustling cities, believing their lives were inherently more exciting. Her grandmother, a woman who had weathered immense hardships in her life, listened to Emma's complaints one afternoon. She then said, with a quiet strength, "The most beautiful roses often grow in the harshest deserts." Emma didn't grasp the meaning at first, but years later, she realized how the challenges of her youth had forged in her a resilience and compassion she might never have developed in an easier environment.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Roses that grow in deserts are scientifically proven to be more beautiful.
Great strength and character can be forged through adversity and difficult circumstances.
One should not complain about their situation but try to make the best of it.
Beauty can be found in the most unexpected places.
Hardships are a necessary prerequisite for a successful life.
questions.explanation: 역경과 어려운 환경을 통해 위대한 힘과 인격이 형성될 수 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Locked Door
passages.text: Ryan was perpetually searching for shortcuts, convinced there had to be an easy path to success. He envied classmates who seemed to achieve greatness without any apparent effort, and he often complained to his father about the unfairness of it all. His father, a self-made man, listened to his son's frustrations. He then stated a simple, unyielding truth: "There are no shortcuts to any place worth going." Ryan initially resisted the idea, clinging to his desire for a simpler way. However, when he finally committed to consistent, hard work, he discovered that the satisfaction from his earned achievements was far more profound than any easy victory could have been.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: All valuable destinations are physically far away and difficult to reach.
Meaningful achievements and true success require dedicated effort and cannot be reached through easy methods.
The easiest path is often the most dangerous one.
One should be suspicious of anything that seems too easy.
The journey is more important than the destination.
questions.explanation: 의미 있는 성취는 헌신적인 노력이 필요하며 쉬운 방법으로는 도달할 수 없다는 의미.

passages.title: The Wounded Bird
passages.text: Jessica found a small bird with a broken wing huddled in her backyard. Her immediate instinct was to help it, to capture it and tend to its injury. But every time she approached, the frightened bird would flutter away painfully, despite its wound. Frustrated, Jessica asked her mother why the bird wouldn't accept her help. Her mother watched the struggling creature for a moment, then said softly, "Sometimes, true love means letting go, even when it hurts to watch." Jessica realized that her desire to control the situation might be causing more harm. Instead of trying to capture it, she left food and water nearby, respecting the bird's need for space and freedom.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: If you truly love something, you must set it free.
Genuine care sometimes requires respecting another's autonomy, even if it's difficult to witness their struggle.
Injured animals should be observed from a distance rather than captured.
Love is an inherently painful emotion.
The desire to help can sometimes be selfish.
questions.explanation: 진정한 보살핌은 때로 상대의 자율성을 존중하는 것을 요구한다는 의미.

passages.title: The Potter's Wheel
passages.text: Daniel, a novice potter, grew deeply frustrated with his clumsy first attempts. He yearned to create the elegant vases that his teacher crafted with such ease, but his hands lacked the necessary skill. On the verge of quitting, he lamented that he simply had no talent for the art. His teacher, without a word, took a shapeless lump of wet clay and slammed it onto the wheel. As it spun, she said, "The clay must be broken down and centered before it can become something beautiful." Daniel understood. His current failures weren't an end, but a necessary, formative part of the learning process itself.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: To create pottery, one must first prepare the clay properly.
One must undergo a process of deconstruction and realignment before true growth can occur.
Failure is an essential ingredient for eventual success in any art form.
The initial stages of learning are always the most difficult and frustrating.
True talent lies in the ability to start over after a failure.
questions.explanation: 진정한 성장을 위해서는 해체와 재정렬의 과정이 필요하다는 의미.

passages.title: The Compass
passages.text: Sarah felt utterly lost and overwhelmed in her vast new high school. With no friends and a bewildering array of clubs and activities, she didn't know where to begin. She confided in her older brother, who saw her paralysis of choice. He offered a simple piece of advice he'd learned from their grandfather. "You don't need to see the whole staircase to take the first step." Taking this to heart, Sarah decided to ignore the overwhelming big picture and simply join one activity that seemed interesting: the photography club. This single step led to new friendships and a growing sense of confidence that made the rest of the staircase seem far less daunting.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is unsafe to climb a staircase without seeing its entirety.
To achieve a large goal, one should focus on the immediate, manageable action rather than the intimidating final outcome.
The first step of any journey is always the most significant.
Progress should be made incrementally, one step at a time.
Overthinking can prevent one from taking necessary action.
questions.explanation: 큰 목표를 달성하려면 최종 결과에 압도되지 말고 즉각적이고 관리 가능한 행동에 집중해야 함.

passages.title: The Seed's Journey
passages.text: Mark, a young and impatient gardener, was disappointed. Weeks after planting his garden, there was no visible sign of life, just barren soil. He complained to his grandfather that all his effort had been a waste of time. His grandfather simply picked up a spare seed from his pocket, held it in his palm, and said with a knowing smile, "What grows in darkness will eventually shine in the light." Mark was puzzled by the cryptic remark. But months later, when his garden erupted in a riot of color and life, he finally understood that the most critical and foundational growth happens unseen, beneath the surface.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Plants that grow in the dark are stronger than those that grow in the sun.
Periods of unseen preparation and struggle are essential for future visible success.
It is important to have faith during difficult times when progress is not apparent.
The beginning of any project is always characterized by a lack of results.
True growth is a slow and gradual process.
questions.explanation: 보이지 않는 준비와 노력의 기간이 미래의 가시적인 성공에 필수적이라는 의미.

passages.title: The Bridge Builder
passages.text: Anna was terrified of public speaking, consistently finding ways to avoid presentations at work. An important conference was approaching where she was expected to speak, and she was on the verge of feigning illness to get out of it. Her coach, sensing her panic, sat her down. "Anna," he said, "everyone feels fear." He then added, "Courage is not the absence of fear; it's walking across the bridge while your knees are shaking." The image resonated with her. Despite her pounding heart and trembling hands, Anna delivered her speech, discovering that bravery wasn't about not feeling afraid, but about acting in spite of it.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: True courage is defined as acting despite feeling fear, not the lack of fear itself.
It is dangerous to cross a bridge if one feels physically unstable.
Overcoming fear is the first step to building courage.
Everyone experiences fear, but only brave people can hide it.
Courage is a skill that can be developed through practice.
questions.explanation: 진정한 용기는 두려움이 없는 상태가 아니라, 두려움을 느끼면서도 행동하는 것임.

passages.title: The Weaver's Pattern
passages.text: Tom felt bitter and resentful about the constant stream of challenges in his life. His friends, it seemed, sailed through life with ease while he was always struggling. He confided his frustrations to his wise aunt, a master weaver. She led him to her loom and showed him the back of a complex tapestry she was working on. It was a chaotic mess of tangled threads and knots. Then, she flipped it over to reveal the stunning, intricate masterpiece on the front. "From the back, you only see tangled threads," she explained, "but from the front, you see the grand design." Tom began to suspect that his own struggles might be part of a larger, meaningful pattern he just couldn't see yet.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A different perspective can reveal the hidden beauty in chaos.
Current hardships and struggles may be integral parts of a larger, meaningful purpose that is not yet apparent.
It is important to focus on the final outcome rather than the messy process.
Life is a combination of both chaotic and beautiful moments.
One must look at things from the correct side to appreciate them.
questions.explanation: 현재의 고난이 아직 보이지 않는 더 큰 의미 있는 목적의 일부일 수 있다는 것.

vocabulary.meaning: 태피스트리(여러 가지 색실로 그림을 짜 넣은 직물)

passages.title: The Mountain Echo
passages.text: Lisa had a habit of criticizing others and spreading negative gossip about her classmates. She couldn't understand why people seemed to avoid her, leaving her feeling isolated and lonely. One day, her teacher overheard her making another sharp complaint about a classmate. The teacher didn't scold her, but instead asked her a question. "Lisa, have you ever shouted into a valley?" She then added, "What you send out into the world comes back to you, like an echo." The simple analogy struck Lisa profoundly. She realized that the negativity and unfriendliness she was experiencing were merely reflections of her own actions and attitude.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Shouting in a valley will produce an echo.
One's actions and attitudes toward others will ultimately be reflected back upon oneself.
Negative comments travel quickly and can harm one's reputation.
The world is a fair place where people get what they deserve.
To receive positivity, one must first be positive.
questions.explanation: 타인에 대한 자신의 행동과 태도는 결국 자신에게 되돌아온다는 의미.

passages.title: The Gardener's Secret
passages.text: Ben was perplexed. His neighbor's garden was always a vibrant tapestry of color, far more beautiful than his own, even though he used the same expensive fertilizers and followed the same gardening techniques from books. Finally, he swallowed his pride and asked his neighbor for his secret. The old man smiled warmly, gesturing to his flourishing plants. "I talk to my plants every day," he said, "not because they understand my words, but because they feel my care." Ben came to understand that genuine, consistent attention and affection were the most potent ingredients of all, in gardening as in life.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Talking to plants can help them grow better.
The most crucial element for success is not technique, but genuine love and dedication.
The neighbor believes his plants have the ability to feel emotions.
Emotional connection is more important than material investment.
The secret to success often lies in simple, overlooked actions.
questions.explanation: 성공의 가장 중요한 요소는 기술이 아니라 진정한 사랑과 헌신이라는 것.

passages.title: The Clock's Wisdom
passages.text: Jake was perpetually stressed, always rushing from one task to the next, complaining that there were never enough hours in the day. He felt like he was in a constant battle against time. His grandmother, observing his frantic pace, offered him a cup of tea and some gentle advice. "Time is like water," she mused, "the tighter you grasp it, the more it slips through your fingers." Jake was skeptical at first, but when he tried to consciously relax and prioritize his tasks instead of fighting the clock, he found that he accomplished more with far less anxiety.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to hold water tightly in one's hand.
Trying too hard to control or manage time can lead to inefficiency and stress.
Time, like water, is a precious and essential resource.
One should let time flow naturally without trying to interfere with it.
A relaxed approach to work is more productive than a stressed one.
questions.explanation: 시간을 너무 통제하려 하면 비효율과 스트레스를 낳는다는 의미.

passages.title: The Artist's Canvas
passages.text: Emily was a perfectionist, an artist who would discard any painting that contained the slightest flaw. Her studio was filled with crumpled canvases, evidence of her frustration. She felt she could never create the perfect work she envisioned. Her art teacher, visiting one day, picked up one of her "failed" paintings, a beautiful landscape marred by a single, accidental streak of black paint. "In art, as in life," the teacher said softly, "the cracks are where the light gets in." Emily learned to embrace her imperfections, discovering that they often added a unique character and depth to her work that perfection never could.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Light can physically enter through cracks in an object.
Flaws and imperfections can unexpectedly become sources of beauty and insight.
It is through our mistakes that we learn the most valuable lessons.
True art is about accepting things as they are, not forcing them to be perfect.
A positive perspective can turn a negative situation into a positive one.
questions.explanation: 결점과 불완전함이 예상치 못하게 아름다움과 통찰의 원천이 될 수 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Tree's Lesson
passages.text: David was consumed by jealousy. His friend had just received a major promotion, and David couldn't help but compare his own slower career path, feeling inadequate and unhappy. His father, noticing his son's gloom, took him for a walk in a dense forest. He pointed to a towering oak standing next to a slender pine. "The oak doesn't try to be a pine, and the pine doesn't envy the oak," his father observed. "Each has its own purpose and its own beauty." In the quiet of the forest, David realized the futility of his comparisons. He had to focus on his own unique growth, not someone else's.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Oak trees and pine trees are fundamentally different species.
One should accept and appreciate one's own unique qualities and path instead of comparing oneself to others.
In nature, different species coexist without competition.
True happiness comes from understanding one's purpose in life.
Trying to be someone you are not will only lead to unhappiness.
questions.explanation: 타인과 비교하기보다 자신만의 고유한 자질과 길을 받아들이고 감사해야 함.

passages.title: The Fisherman's Net
passages.text: Carlos, an impatient young fisherman, grew increasingly frustrated as the hours passed without a single bite. Convinced the fish were simply in a different part of the vast lake, he constantly pulled up his anchor and moved his boat, leaving ripples of agitation in his wake. His experienced grandfather, sitting calmly in the same spot since dawn, watched his grandson's restless movements with a knowing sigh. After Carlos had moved for the fifth time, the old man called out across the water, his voice steady and clear, "A fisherman who chases the fish all day will only come home with an empty net and a tired heart." Carlos paused, the wisdom of the words sinking in. He learned that day that success in fishing, and perhaps in life, often comes not from frantic searching, but from patient, focused waiting.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Constantly changing one's strategy without patience leads only to exhaustion and failure.
A good fisherman must know the right spots to find fish.
Chasing after something aggressively is less effective than waiting for it.
Fishing requires a calm and peaceful state of mind.
An empty net is a sign of a fisherman's lack of skill.
questions.explanation: 인내심 없이 계속 전략을 바꾸면 피로와 실패만 남는다는 의미.

passages.title: The Musician's Practice
passages.text: Maya, a dedicated piano student, dreamed of playing complex concertos like a professional. However, she often felt discouraged when her daily practice sessions didn't yield immediate, dramatic results. She complained to her teacher that her slow progress felt insignificant. Her teacher, a wise old maestro, didn't offer words of comfort. Instead, he led her to a window overlooking a small stream that trickled down the mountain. "A single raindrop makes no sound in the river," he said, "but enough of them together can carve a canyon." Maya understood. Each seemingly small practice session was a single drop, contributing to a powerful, cumulative force that would eventually shape her mastery.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The sound of a single raindrop is too small to be heard.
Consistent, small efforts, though seemingly minor on their own, accumulate to create significant and powerful results over time.
Great achievements are the result of many people working together.
The power of nature is a good metaphor for the process of learning music.
Patience is required to see the results of one's hard work.
questions.explanation: 작고 꾸준한 노력이 쌓여 시간이 지나면 중요하고 강력한 결과를 만들어낸다는 것.

passages.title: The Sculptor's Vision
passages.text: Jason, a student of woodworking, became deeply frustrated. The figures he tried to carve never matched the grand visions in his imagination; his creations were clumsy and lifeless. Convinced he lacked any innate artistic talent, he was ready to abandon the craft. His mentor, a master carver, took a rough, gnarled piece of driftwood. He turned it over in his hands, studying its grain and contours. "I do not force a shape onto the wood," the mentor explained softly. "I listen to the wood and simply free the sculpture that is already waiting inside." Jason learned to work *with* the material's natural character, discovering that true artistry was a partnership, not a dictatorship.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Every piece of wood contains a pre-existing sculpture.
The artist's role is to discover and reveal the inherent potential within a medium, rather than imposing a foreign idea upon it.
One must have a clear vision before starting a creative project.
The natural form of a material is always more beautiful than an artificial one.
True talent involves listening to one's intuition.
questions.explanation: 예술가의 역할은 매체의 내재된 잠재력을 발견하고 드러내는 것이라는 의미.

passages.title: The Garden's Teaching
passages.text: Sophie was consumed with envy for her neighbor's breathtakingly beautiful garden. She wanted the same spectacular results, and she wanted them immediately. She spared no expense, planting the most expensive flowers and using high-tech irrigation systems. But her garden remained a pale imitation. Her wise neighbor, observing her frantic efforts, offered a gentle piece of advice over the fence one day. "A garden is not built in a day, my dear. It is woven together with days." Sophie began to understand that a truly beautiful garden wasn't a project to be completed, but a relationship to be nurtured through daily, patient care and observation.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It takes more than one day to build a garden.
Lasting and beautiful results are achieved through consistent, cumulative daily effort, not a single, intense burst of activity.
Gardening is a process that unfolds over many seasons.
The passage of time is the most important ingredient for a beautiful garden.
One should focus on the process rather than the final product.
questions.explanation: 지속적이고 아름다운 결과는 꾸준한 일상의 노력으로 이루어진다는 것.

passages.title: The Candle's Wisdom
passages.text: Peter, a brilliant but insecure analyst, was terrified of sharing his innovative ideas during team meetings. He harbored a deep-seated fear that his colleagues might steal his concepts and claim them as their own. This fear kept him silent and isolated, his potential locked away. His manager, a perceptive leader, noticed his reluctance to contribute. In a private coaching session, she told him, "A candle's flame is not diminished when it is used to light another. In fact, it creates more light." The simple metaphor illuminated Peter's fear for what it was: irrational. He realized sharing his ideas would not reduce his own worth, but rather amplify the collective intelligence of the team.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A candle's flame can be shared without any loss to itself.
Sharing knowledge and ideas enhances the collective good without diminishing the individual's value.
Collaboration leads to better outcomes than individual work.
Fear of being copied should not prevent one from being creative.
The purpose of a good idea is to be shared with others.
questions.explanation: 지식과 아이디어를 나누는 것은 개인의 가치를 줄이지 않고 집단의 선을 향상시킨다는 의미.

passages.title: The River's Journey
passages.text: Amanda felt her life was in ruins. Her carefully constructed five-year plan had been completely derailed by an unexpected company downsizing. The career path she had meticulously mapped out had vanished overnight. Feeling lost and powerless, she confided in her therapist. The therapist listened to her story of frustration and then offered a perspective shift. "A river that encounters a boulder does not stop flowing," she said. "It simply finds a new path around it on its journey to the sea." Amanda began to see that her rigid plan was the problem, not the unexpected obstacle. She learned to value adaptability, eventually finding a more fulfilling career in a related but different field.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Rivers are powerful enough to overcome any obstacle in their path.
When faced with an insurmountable obstacle, one must find an alternative route to achieve one's ultimate goal.
It is futile to resist the natural flow of life's events.
Flexibility and resilience are more valuable than rigid planning.
Every journey, like a river's, eventually reaches its destination.
questions.explanation: 경직된 계획보다 유연성과 회복력이 더 가치 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Seed's Faith
passages.text: Michael felt the immense weight of expectation as he started his first year of college. Coming from a low-income family, he was the first to pursue higher education, and the four-year journey to a degree seemed like an insurmountable mountain. He was overwhelmed by anxiety, constantly worrying about the final outcome. His academic advisor, sensing his distress, gave him a single acorn. "A seed does not need to see the whole forest to begin its journey of growth," she told him. Michael understood. He needed to stop worrying about the entire degree and focus on what was right in front of him: taking one class, reading one chapter, and passing one exam at a time.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A seed can grow without being aware of the larger ecosystem.
One should begin a large undertaking by focusing on the immediate first steps, without being intimidated by the final goal.
Great things often have very small and humble beginnings.
Faith in oneself is essential for starting a long journey.
The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
questions.explanation: 큰 과업을 시작할 때는 최종 목표에 위축되지 말고 즉각적인 첫걸음에 집중해야 함.

passages.title: The Mirror's Truth
passages.text: Rachel lived in a state of constant anxiety, her self-worth entirely dependent on the approval of others. She meticulously curated her social media presence and altered her personality to fit what she thought her friends wanted to see. She felt exhausted from the performance. Her mentor, a wise woman who had known her for years, observed this behavior with concern. During a quiet conversation, she told Rachel, "You will never see your own true reflection if you are always looking in other people's mirrors." The statement struck Rachel with the force of truth. She realized she had to turn her gaze inward and cultivate a sense of self independent of external validation.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to see oneself while looking at someone else.
Relying solely on the opinions of others for self-worth prevents one from discovering their authentic self.
To truly understand yourself, you must spend time in solitude.
Other people's perceptions of you are often distorted and unreliable.
Self-awareness is the key to building genuine confidence.
questions.explanation: 타인의 의견에만 의존하면 진정한 자아를 발견할 수 없다는 의미.

passages.title: The Clock's Teaching
passages.text: Nathan was a young entrepreneur, always rushing, perpetually stressed, and convinced that 24 hours in a day was a fundamental design flaw of the universe. He complained constantly about his lack of time. His grandfather, a retired craftsman who lived a remarkably peaceful and productive life, listened to his grandson's frantic complaints. He then said calmly, "The challenge is not to get more hours in your day, but to get more life in your hours." Nathan began to understand that the issue wasn't the quantity of time, but the quality of his attention and the wisdom of his priorities. He learned to make his hours count, rather than just counting them.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The quality and meaningfulness of how time is spent are more important than the sheer quantity of time.
It is impossible to increase the number of hours in a day.
A fulfilling life is measured by experiences, not by duration.
Effective time management is about setting the right priorities.
One should focus on living in the present moment.
questions.explanation: 시간의 양보다 시간을 어떻게 의미 있게 보내는지가 더 중요하다는 것.

passages.title: The Eagle's Flight
passages.text: Christina was paralyzed by her fear of failure. She consistently chose safe, familiar options in her career and personal life, deliberately avoiding any challenge that carried the risk of defeat. Her coach, frustrated by her stagnant potential, tried a new approach to motivate her. He pointed to a documentary playing on a nearby screen, showing a mother eagle nudging her fledgling out of the nest. "An eagle does not learn to fly by studying the view from the nest," the coach said pointedly. Christina realized that true growth and mastery could only be achieved by leaving her comfort zone and embracing the very risks she feared.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: An eagle must leave its nest to learn how to fly.
Growth and learning require taking risks and moving beyond one's area of comfort and safety.
Theoretical knowledge is useless without practical application.
The nest represents a place of safety that ultimately hinders development.
Courage is necessary to face new challenges.
questions.explanation: 성장과 학습은 안전지대를 벗어나 위험을 감수할 때 이루어진다는 의미.

passages.title: The Blacksmith's Fire
passages.text: Oliver, a young apprentice blacksmith, complained incessantly about the searing heat of the forge and the grueling labor of hammering metal. He was convinced there must be an easier, more clever way to shape the steel. His master, a man of few words and powerful arms, listened to his complaints in silence. One day, he took a piece of untreated, brittle iron and a piece of finely tempered steel. He handed both to Oliver. "Steel that has never known the fire remains forever weak," the master stated simply. Oliver finally understood that the intense, difficult process he so despised was not a punishment, but the very thing that transformed weak iron into strong, valuable steel.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Steel becomes strong only after being subjected to intense heat.
Hardship and difficult challenges are necessary to build strength and character.
The process of blacksmithing is inherently difficult and requires great endurance.
Weak materials cannot withstand the pressures of the forge.
One must embrace difficult processes to create valuable things.
questions.explanation: 고난과 어려운 도전은 힘과 인격을 형성하는 데 필요하다는 의미.

passages.title: The Teacher's Legacy
passages.text: Ms. Park often felt a deep sense of futility teaching in an underfunded school with students facing immense challenges. Progress was slow, and appreciation was rare. She seriously contemplated leaving the profession, wondering if her tireless efforts were ultimately worthless. Years later, a former student, now a successful architect, visited her. He explained how her belief in him had been the foundation of his success. That evening, as she walked through the quiet school garden she had planted years ago, a thought crystallized in her mind: "The trees I water today will one day give shade to children I may never meet." This realization renewed her sense of purpose, anchoring her once more to her calling.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Planting trees is a long-term investment for the future.
The positive influence of one's current efforts can have far-reaching, unseen benefits for future generations.
A teacher's job is to care for students, much like a gardener cares for trees.
The full impact of education is often not realized by the teacher herself.
One should not expect immediate results from their hard work.
questions.explanation: 현재 노력의 긍정적 영향은 미래 세대에게 보이지 않는 혜택을 줄 수 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Photographer's Lens
passages.text: Julia, an amateur photographer, was perpetually frustrated. The images she captured with her camera never seemed to match the breathtaking beauty of the scenery she saw with her own eyes. She blamed her entry-level equipment, convinced that an expensive, professional-grade camera was the key to unlocking her potential. Her seasoned instructor, however, showed her a stunning portfolio of photos all taken with a simple smartphone. He then tapped his own temple and said, "The most important part of the camera is the twelve inches behind it." Julia learned that vision, creativity, and the ability to see light and composition were far more critical than the price tag of her gear.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The back part of a camera is its most critical component.
The photographer's skill, vision, and creativity are more important than the quality of the equipment.
A good photographer can take great pictures with any camera.
One should invest in education and training rather than expensive gear.
The distance between the photographer and the subject is crucial.
questions.explanation: 사진가의 기술, 비전, 창의성이 장비의 질보다 더 중요하다는 의미.

passages.title: The Baker's Patience
passages.text: Marcus poured his life savings into opening his dream bakery. He was a talented baker, but an impatient businessman. He expected customers to be lining up from the very first day. When business was initially slow, a deep anxiety began to set in, and he considered cutting corners or even giving up. His mentor, a retired baker with flour dust seemingly etched into his skin, visited the quiet shop. He watched Marcus pace nervously, then said calmly, "You cannot command the bread to rise; you can only give it the warmth and time it needs." Marcus learned that building a loyal customer base, like baking good bread, required patience and consistent quality, not frantic commands.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The process of bread rising is controlled by natural factors like time and temperature.
Certain processes cannot be rushed and require patience and the right conditions to unfold naturally.
A successful business grows organically over time.
One should not try to force results but rather create an environment where success can grow.
Patience is the most important virtue for a baker and a businessman.
questions.explanation: 결과를 강요하기보다 성공이 자랄 수 있는 환경을 조성해야 한다는 것.

passages.title: The Mountain Climber's Wisdom
passages.text: Sarah, on her first major mountain expedition, found herself constantly staring up at the distant, intimidating summit. The sheer scale of the remaining climb felt overwhelming, and a sense of hopelessness began to creep in. She felt her resolve wavering and was tempted to turn back. Her experienced guide, noticing her gaze fixed on the peak and her slowing pace, came to her side. He didn't offer words of encouragement about the summit. Instead, he pointed to the patch of rock directly in front of her boots. "Do not try to climb the mountain," he advised. "Simply climb the step in front of you." Sarah shifted her focus, and with each conquered step, she found the strength to continue.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: One should focus on the immediate, manageable task at hand rather than being overwhelmed by the larger goal.
Mountain climbing is a step-by-step process.
It is more practical to focus on the journey than the destination.
Large goals can be broken down into smaller, more achievable parts.
Looking too far ahead can lead to discouragement and failure.
questions.explanation: 압도적인 목표는 큰 목표에 압도되지 않고 당면한 과제에 집중함으로써 달성된다는 의미.

passages.title: The Writer's Block
passages.text: For months, Emma had been trapped in the paralyzing grip of writer's block. She would sit at her desk for hours, but every sentence she managed to write seemed clumsy and inadequate. She would delete everything in frustration, leaving behind a glaringly blank page. Convinced her talent had vanished, she was ready to abandon her novel. Her writing coach, after listening to her despair, offered a blunt but transformative piece of advice. "You can't edit a blank page," he said. Emma realized her quest for perfection was preventing her from even starting. She needed to allow herself to write an imperfect first draft, a messy foundation that she could later shape and refine.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to edit a page that has no text on it.
To improve something, one must first create something, even if it is imperfect.
The fear of imperfection is the greatest obstacle to creativity.
The writing process involves multiple stages, including drafting and editing.
It is better to have a flawed draft than no draft at all.
questions.explanation: 무언가를 개선하려면, 비록 불완전하더라도 먼저 무언가를 만들어야 한다는 것.

passages.title: The Gardener's Philosophy
passages.text: Robert was consumed by a friendly but intense rivalry with his neighbor, whose roses were always the talk of the neighborhood. He tried every technique, every exotic fertilizer, and every pruning method he could read about, yet his roses remained merely average. One evening, he finally asked his neighbor for his ultimate secret. The neighbor, a man with soil permanently under his fingernails, smiled. "The secret ingredient isn't in the soil or the water," he confided. "It's in the shadow the gardener casts upon the plants each day." Robert understood. The secret wasn't a product or a technique, but the consistent, loving presence and devotion of the gardener himself.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The gardener's shadow provides necessary shade for the roses.
The most critical factor for success is the gardener's consistent, personal attention and care.
The best gardening secrets are not written in books.
Passion and love are more important than any specific technique.
Success comes from daily, dedicated practice.
questions.explanation: 성공의 가장 중요한 요소는 정원사의 꾸준한 개인적 관심과 보살핌이라는 의미.

passages.title: The Musician's Journey
passages.text: Kevin, a diligent piano student, practiced for hours every day. Yet, he often felt a crushing sense of inadequacy whenever he listened to recordings of professional musicians or attended concerts. The gap between his current ability and their mastery seemed impossibly wide, and he wondered if he could ever be "good enough." He expressed these frustrations to his teacher. His teacher listened patiently, then said, "Do not compare your chapter one to someone else's chapter twenty." The analogy resonated deeply with Kevin. He realized that every master was once a beginner, and he needed to focus on his own unique journey and progress, not on an unfair comparison.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is illogical to compare one's beginning stages with someone else's advanced stage.
Everyone's journey of learning and development is different and proceeds at its own pace.
Comparing oneself to others is often a source of unnecessary frustration and discouragement.
One should focus on their own progress rather than on the achievements of others.
The first chapter of a book is fundamentally different from the last.
questions.explanation: 자신의 시작 단계를 다른 사람의 고급 단계와 비교하는 것은 비논리적이라는 것.

passages.title: The Lighthouse Keeper's Duty
passages.text: George kept the lighthouse on a remote, windswept island. Weeks would go by with only a few ships passing in the distant sea lanes. He often felt a profound sense of loneliness, questioning if his solitary, repetitive work truly mattered in an age of GPS. During a hurricane of historic intensity, the island's power failed, but George kept the light's massive lens turning by hand all night. The next morning, a grateful coast guard captain radioed his thanks; the light had been the only beacon for a rescue boat in the storm. George's friend later remarked, "Your light may not be needed by every ship, but for the one that is lost in the dark, it is everything."

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The lighthouse's primary function is to help ships that are in trouble.
The value of one's work should not be measured by its frequency, but by its impact in critical moments.
Even a single act of service can have a profound and life-saving impact.
One's duty may seem insignificant most of the time but can become crucial unexpectedly.
It is important to be prepared for emergencies.
questions.explanation: 일의 가치는 빈도가 아니라 결정적인 순간의 영향력으로 측정되어야 한다는 의미.

passages.title: The Artist's Canvas
passages.text: Lisa was a talented painter but a crippling perfectionist. She would abandon a canvas if a single brushstroke felt out of place or a color was not the exact shade she had envisioned. Consequently, her studio was a graveyard of unfinished works, and she felt deeply unproductive. Her art teacher, observing her struggle, picked up one of the "failed" paintings. He then took a brush and made a bold, seemingly random stroke across it. "Sometimes," he said, "the most beautiful accidents happen when we stop trying to control every single outcome." Lisa learned to embrace the unexpected, finding that surrendering a little control could open the door to a new, more dynamic form of creativity.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Accidents can sometimes improve a work of art.
Relinquishing absolute control can lead to unexpected and valuable creative discoveries.
Perfectionism is a major obstacle to artistic productivity.
True creativity involves an element of randomness and chance.
It is important to be open to making mistakes in the creative process.
questions.explanation: 절대적인 통제를 포기하면 예상치 못한 가치 있는 창의적 발견으로 이어질 수 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Mason's Stone
passages.text: David, an apprentice mason, was growing disheartened. No matter how carefully he cut his stones, they never seemed to fit together as perfectly as his master's work. His walls looked rough and amateurish. Convinced he was simply not suited for the trade, he told his master he wanted to quit. The master mason picked up an irregularly shaped, seemingly flawed stone that David had discarded. He expertly fitted it into a gap in the wall, where it sat perfectly. "Every stone has its place in the wall," the master said, "even the ones that appear broken." David learned that perceived imperfections could, with skill, contribute to the overall strength and character of the whole.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Broken stones can still be used in construction.
Every individual, despite their perceived flaws or differences, has a valuable role to play within a larger community or structure.
A skilled mason can make any stone fit perfectly.
Imperfections should be embraced as they add unique character.
The strength of a wall depends on using a variety of stones.
questions.explanation: 결함에도 불구하고 모든 개인은 더 큰 공동체 내에서 가치 있는 역할을 할 수 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Runner's Pace
passages.text: Michelle was training for her first marathon and became obsessed with the times of professional runners, which she followed online. The vast gap between their pace and her own left her feeling deeply discouraged and inadequate, making her question why she was even trying. Her coach noticed her dwindling motivation and the logbook where she compared her times to the elites. He sat her down and pointed to her own previous entries. "You are not racing against them," he stated firmly. "You are only racing against the person you were yesterday." This shift in perspective freed Michelle from the trap of comparison, allowing her to find joy and motivation in her own personal improvement.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The true measure of progress is self-improvement, not comparison with others.
Marathon running is primarily an individual sport.
One should aim to beat their own personal records.
Comparing oneself to professionals is an unrealistic and demotivating practice.
Focusing on the past can help motivate future performance.
questions.explanation: 진정한 진보의 척도는 타인과의 비교가 아닌 자기 개선이라는 것.

passages.title: The Painter's Mistake
passages.text: Alex was meticulously painting a serene landscape when his hand slipped, leaving a large, ugly smudge of dark paint right across the tranquil sky. His heart sank. The entire composition was ruined. He was about to discard the canvas in disgust when his art teacher intervened. She studied the smudge for a long moment, a thoughtful expression on her face. "Sometimes, what we call a mistake," she mused, "is simply an opportunity in disguise." Inspired by her words, Alex didn't try to cover the smudge. Instead, he transformed it into the heart of a dramatic, swirling storm cloud, creating a painting far more powerful and dynamic than his original, placid vision.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: An apparent mistake or accident can be transformed into a positive and creative element.
Great art often arises from unplanned accidents.
It is important not to be discouraged by one's errors.
A true artist can find a use for any mistake.
A change in perspective can turn a problem into a solution.
questions.explanation: 명백한 실수나 사고가 긍정적이고 창의적인 요소로 변형될 수 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Farmer's Harvest
passages.text: Having inherited his family's farm, James was driven by a desire for quick profits. He ignored the time-honored traditions of soil preparation, opting instead for fast-growing crops and heavy chemical fertilizers. He believed modern shortcuts could bypass the slow rhythms of nature. When his harvest was meager and the soil was left depleted, he blamed the unpredictable weather. An old, experienced farmer from the next property, having observed James's methods all season, offered a quiet piece of wisdom. "You cannot hope to harvest in the autumn what you did not sow in the spring." James realized his failure wasn't due to bad luck, but to his own lack of foundational work and patience.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: One must plant seeds in the spring to have a harvest in the autumn.
Desired outcomes cannot be achieved without first putting in the necessary foundational effort and preparation.
Shortcuts and quick fixes often lead to poor long-term results.
One should not blame external factors for failures caused by one's own actions.
Success is a direct result of the work that is invested.
questions.explanation: 원하는 결과는 필요한 기초 노력과 준비 없이는 달성될 수 없다는 것.

vocabulary.meaning: 오랜 세월에 걸쳐 존중받아 온, 전통적인

passages.title: The Sculptor's Patience
passages.text: Rosa was sculpting her first life-sized statue and was consumed by an overwhelming desire to see it finished. This impatience made her rush through crucial stages, leading to several small but significant errors in proportion and detail. Her instructor, a master sculptor renowned for his meticulous work, watched her frantic efforts with a calm demeanor. He didn't criticize her mistakes directly. Instead, he handed her a very fine, delicate chisel. "In our craft," he said, "patience is the sharpest tool." Rosa slowed down, understanding that haste was dulling her skills, and that careful, deliberate work was the only way to achieve the quality she desired.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A sculptor's most important tool is a sharp chisel.
Patience is the most essential quality for achieving high-quality, detailed work in a craft.
Rushing through a project inevitably leads to errors.
The quality of one's tools can affect the quality of one's work.
True mastery requires not just skill, but also the right temperament.
questions.explanation: 인내심은 고품질의 세밀한 작업을 달성하는 데 가장 필수적인 자질이라는 의미.

passages.title: The Teacher's Impact
passages.text: Mrs. Lee taught in a challenging, low-income neighborhood where progress was often slow and setbacks were frequent. She poured her heart into her work, but often felt her efforts were like stones dropped into a bottomless well—unnoticed and unappreciated. After many years, feeling burnt out, she considered a career change. That week, she received a flood of emails and letters, organized by a former student for Teacher Appreciation Week. They were from students she had taught decades ago, now doctors, artists, and community leaders, all citing her influence as a turning point. As she read them, she finally understood: "A teacher's influence does not stop at the classroom door; it echoes in eternity."

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The lessons learned in a classroom are remembered for a lifetime.
The positive impact of a teacher is profound and continues to affect students long after they have left school.
A good teacher's reputation can last forever.
The true value of education is immeasurable and timeless.
A teacher's work has consequences that extend far into the future.
questions.explanation: 교사의 긍정적 영향은 학생이 학교를 떠난 후에도 오랫동안 계속된다는 것.

passages.title: The Gardener's Vision
passages.text: Tom planted his first garden with a perfect, orderly picture from a magazine in his mind. When the actual garden began to grow, it seemed chaotic and messy, nothing like his idealized vision. The plants grew at different rates, and weeds popped up in unexpected places. Deeply disappointed, he complained to his experienced neighbor. The neighbor looked over the thriving, if somewhat unruly, patch of green. "A garden is not a static painting," he chuckled. "It is a slow-motion dance, choreographed by the seasons." Tom learned to let go of his rigid expectations and began to appreciate the dynamic, ever-changing beauty of a living garden.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A garden's appearance changes with the seasons.
One should embrace the natural, dynamic, and sometimes unpredictable process of growth rather than imposing rigid expectations.
A real garden can never be as perfect as a picture.
The beauty of a garden lies in its constant transformation.
Patience is required to see a garden develop over time.
questions.explanation: 경직된 기대를 강요하기보다 자연스럽고 역동적인 성장 과정을 받아들여야 한다는 의미.

passages.title: The Writer's Voice
passages.text: Emma deeply admired the works of several famous authors and tried to emulate their sophisticated styles in her own writing. However, her stories always felt artificial and forced, lacking a genuine core. She grew frustrated, feeling that her own voice was simply not good enough. Her writing mentor read one of her derivative pieces and offered a crucial insight. "You are trying to sing in someone else's voice," he told her. "But the world is waiting to hear the song that only you can sing." Emma stopped trying to imitate others and began the difficult but rewarding journey of discovering and developing her own unique narrative style.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to perfectly imitate another author's style.
True creativity and value lie in expressing one's own unique perspective and style, not in copying others.
The world values originality more than imitation.
Finding one's own voice is the most important goal for a writer.
One should be confident in their own abilities.
questions.explanation: 진정한 창의성과 가치는 타인을 모방하는 것이 아니라 자신만의 고유한 관점을 표현하는 데 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Fisherman's Wisdom
passages.text: Lucas was an impatient fisherman, constantly fidgeting with his gear and moving his boat every few minutes, convinced that constant action would yield results. His grandfather, who had been fishing the same lake for fifty years, watched his grandson's restless energy with a serene smile. The old man hadn't moved for hours, yet his bucket was half-full. "The fish do not come to the thrashing water," he finally said, his voice as calm as the lake's deep center. "They come to the quiet line." Lucas learned that in fishing, as in many pursuits, a state of calm focus and patience is often more effective than restless, frantic activity.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Fish are scared away by loud noises and sudden movements.
A calm, patient, and focused approach is more likely to lead to success than anxious, constant activity.
To be a good fisherman, one must learn to be still and quiet.
Over-eagerness can be counterproductive.
Wisdom comes from years of experience.
questions.explanation: 불안하고 조급한 마음가짐은 성공을 방해할 수 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Clock Maker's Craft
passages.text: Peter, a young apprentice to a master clockmaker, was frustrated by the painstaking, intricate details required for the craft. He saw the tiny gears and springs as tedious obstacles and often tried to skip steps to finish a timepiece faster. His master, after examining a clock Peter had hastily assembled, pointed to its erratic ticking. "Time itself is our greatest teacher," the master said gravely. "It teaches us that precision cannot be rushed." Peter realized that the very nature of what he was trying to measure—time—demanded a slow, deliberate, and precise approach. Quality, he learned, was a function of patience.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The concept of time demonstrates that accuracy requires a slow and careful process.
High-quality, precise work cannot be achieved through haste and shortcuts.
A good clockmaker must have a deep respect for time.
The process of creating something should reflect the nature of the thing being created.
Patience and attention to detail are the keys to mastery in any craft.
questions.explanation: 고품질의 정밀한 작업은 서두름과 지름길을 통해 달성될 수 없다는 것.

passages.title: The Conductor's Orchestra
passages.text: Ryan, a talented violinist, recently joined the prestigious school orchestra. However, he quickly grew frustrated because, amidst the thunder of the brass and the swell of the strings, he could barely hear his own individual instrument. He felt his contribution was insignificant, lost in the massive soundscape. Considering quitting, he confided his feelings to the conductor. The wise conductor listened, then tapped his baton for silence at the next rehearsal. "A symphony is not one instrument playing a magnificent solo," he announced to the entire orchestra. "It is the magic of one hundred instruments playing as one." Ryan realized that true harmony wasn't about being heard individually, but about contributing perfectly to the whole.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The loudest instrument is not the most important in an orchestra.
Collective harmony and collaboration are more valuable than individual prominence.
A symphony requires a large number of musicians to be successful.
It is more difficult to play together than to play alone.
Each musician must listen carefully to the conductor's direction.
questions.explanation: 집단적 조화와 협력이 개인의 두각보다 더 가치 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Jeweler's Gem
passages.text: On a beachcombing trip, Kate found a dull, rough-looking stone and was about to toss it back into the waves. Her grandmother, a retired jeweler with a keen eye, stopped her. She took the unremarkable stone, turned it over in the sunlight, and felt its texture. "The most precious diamond," she said with a knowing smile, "spends a million years looking like an ordinary rock. It only reveals its fire when it meets the right hands." Intrigued, Kate took the stone to a local lapidary. After careful cutting and polishing, the "ordinary rock" was revealed to be a stunning, semi-precious agate, which became Kate's most treasured possession.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Diamonds are geologically indistinguishable from common rocks in their raw state.
Hidden potential and value can be revealed through proper skill, effort, and recognition.
It takes a trained expert to identify something truly valuable.
The true worth of an object is not always apparent on the surface.
Patience and time are required to create something of great value.
questions.explanation: 숨겨진 잠재력과 가치는 적절한 기술, 노력, 인식을 통해 드러날 수 있다는 것.

passages.title: The Chef's Recipe
passages.text: Daniel, an ambitious culinary student, dreamed of becoming a celebrity chef overnight. He constantly tried to replicate the most complex and flamboyant dishes from famous television chefs, but his attempts were consistently disastrous. Discouraged and frustrated, he presented his latest failed creation to his instructor. The seasoned chef tasted the dish, sighed, and then offered a piece of fundamental advice. "You are trying to build the roof," he said, "before you have laid the foundation." Humbled, Daniel went back to mastering the basic skills—knife work, sauce making, and heat control—gradually building the foundation upon which he could later construct his culinary masterpieces.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: One is attempting advanced techniques without having mastered the fundamental skills.
It is illogical to build the roof of a house before the foundation.
Ambitious goals require a solid and well-planned base.
Basic skills are more important than complex recipes in cooking.
Success in any field must be built step-by-step.
questions.explanation: 기본 기술을 습득하지 않고 고급 기술을 시도하고 있다는 의미.

passages.title: The Builder's Foundation
passages.text: Mark was excited to build a large treehouse and impatiently ignored his father's detailed advice about laying a strong, level foundation. He wanted to get to the "fun part"—building the walls and the roof—as quickly as possible. He rushed through the foundational work, eager to see his structure take shape. For a week, it looked impressive. Then, after a heavy rainstorm, the entire structure tilted and collapsed. His father, surveying the wreckage, put a hand on his son's shoulder. "A building is only as strong as the parts you cannot see," he said quietly. Mark learned a hard lesson about the critical importance of the unseen, foundational work that supports everything else.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The strength of a building depends on its hidden foundation.
The unseen, foundational elements of any endeavor are the most critical for its long-term stability and success.
One should not judge things based on their outward appearance.
Proper preparation is essential to withstand unexpected challenges.
The most important work is often the least visible and exciting.
questions.explanation: 어떤 노력의 보이지 않는 기초 요소가 장기적 안정과 성공에 가장 중요하다는 것.

passages.title: The Navigator's Compass
passages.text: Sophie, a novice hiking guide, was leading a group through a dense forest. Lacking confidence, she kept second-guessing her decisions. Whenever she encountered a steep hill or a muddy patch, she would change her intended route, hoping to find an easier way. The group became confused, exhausted, and demoralized by the constant changes in direction. An experienced hiker in the group finally approached her privately. "A compass that points in every direction," he said gently but firmly, "is the same as having no compass at all." Sophie understood. She needed to commit to a clear path and navigate obstacles, not abandon her course at the first sign of difficulty.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A broken compass is useless for navigation.
A lack of consistent direction and commitment renders any plan or guidance ineffective.
It is better to face challenges head-on than to constantly try to avoid them.
A good leader must be decisive and confident.
Constantly changing one's mind leads to confusion and failure.
questions.explanation: 일관된 방향성과 헌신이 부족하면 어떤 계획도 효과가 없다는 의미.

passages.title: The Doctor's Patience
passages.text: Jennifer, a first-year medical student, was deeply impatient. She was frustrated by the endless hours of studying dense textbooks on biochemistry and anatomy. She wanted to be in the hospital, helping patients, not memorizing abstract theories. She complained to her professor, a renowned physician, that the theoretical study seemed disconnected from the practical reality of healing. The professor listened, then looked at her over his spectacles. "You cannot hope to heal a body," he said with quiet authority, "whose language you do not yet understand." Jennifer realized that the "boring" theories were the very language of the human body, and a thorough understanding was the non-negotiable prerequisite to responsible healing.

questions.text: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: To heal a body, one must first learn the language of medicine.
A deep and thorough understanding of fundamental principles is essential before one can effectively apply practical skills.
Theoretical knowledge and practical skills are two sides of the same coin.
Impatience can be a dangerous quality in a medical professional.
One must not attempt to solve a problem without fully understanding its nature.
questions.explanation: 기본 원리에 대한 깊은 이해가 실용적 기술을 효과적으로 적용하기 전에 필수적이라는 것.

passages.title: The Dancer's Grace
passages.text: Ashley, a talented but undisciplined ballet student, focused solely on the most dramatic and impressive moves—the high leaps and rapid spins. She neglected the tedious but essential barre exercises and basic positions. While her performances were momentarily spectacular, they lacked control and felt awkward. Her instructor, a former prima ballerina, watched her practice one day. "My dear, you are chasing the thunder," she said, "but grace lives in the quiet rain that follows."
